title: tephrocactus corrugatus
intro: Tephrocactus corrugatus is a small, globular cactus species native to the Americas, characterized by its warty, ribbed stems and fine spines. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Tephrocactus corrugatus include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Tephrocactus corrugatus typically involve infusions of the plant's stems or roots, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
